questions.question: What is the primary reason for using 'lookalike' domains (e.g., getcompany.com) for cold outreach?

options.text: It makes your company look bigger
To protect your primary business domain from being blacklisted by spam filters
It is cheaper than buying a primary domain
Lookalike domains automatically bypass Google's spam filters

questions.explanation: Cold outreach carries an inherent risk of spam complaints. Isolating that risk to secondary domains ensures your primary business communication (invoices, support) remains safe.

questions.question: How many SPF records are you allowed to have on a single domain?
questions.explanation: The SPF specification only allows one record. If multiple records are present, they will all fail authentication. You must merge all authorized senders into a single record string.

questions.question: Which record provides a 'cryptographic seal' to prove the content of an email wasn't tampered with?
questions.explanation: DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail) uses a digital signature verified by a public key in your DNS to ensure message integrity.

questions.question: What does a DMARC policy of 'p=none' tell a receiving server?

options.text: Reject all emails that fail authentication
Deliver the email even if it fails authentication, but send me a report
Put failing emails in the spam folder

questions.explanation: 'p=none' is the monitoring policy. It allows emails to pass through if they fail checks but provides the domain owner with reporting data to fix issues.

questions.question: Which tool is recommended for verifying that your SPF, DKIM, and DMARC are configured correctly?
questions.explanation: MXToolbox is a standard practitioner tool for DNS lookups and authentication verification.

questions.question: Review your current or planned outbound domain setup. List the lookalike domain you've chosen and explain your 'merging' strategy if you use multiple sending tools (e.g., Google Workspace + Instantly). Why is 'p=none' a safe starting point for DMARC?
questions.promptForAI: Verify the domain choice (should be a variation, not the primary). Check the understanding of the one-SPF-record rule. Evaluate the DMARC rationale (it avoids accidental blocking of legitimate mail while setup is finalized).
